high_value_in_scope_signals: <program-slug> Mesh executable; critical eligible; 0 resolved reports
<program-slug>-supported Gateway Plugins executable; critical eligible; 0 resolved reports
<program-slug> Gateway Enterprise executable; critical eligible; 0 resolved reports
Insomnia Desktop Client executable; critical eligible; 2 resolved reports
Insomnia CLI/inso executable; critical eligible; 0 resolved reports
us.identity.<program-redacted>.com OAuth/OIDC endpoints; critical eligible; 0 resolved reports
app.<program-redacted> web app; critical eligible; 15 resolved reports
*.api.<program-redacted>.com Konnect APIs; critical eligible; 0 resolved reports
cloud.<program-redacted>.com Konnect management console; critical eligible; 4 resolved reports
subdomain takeover on *.<program-redacted>.com; high eligible but requires successful control and explicit boundary
notable_policy_focus: Multi-tenancy and authorization: cross-tenant config access, IDOR on services/routes/plugins/certs/runtime_groups, read-only to admin privilege escalation
Stored XSS in developer portal/shared fields
Sensitive data exposure: client secrets/API keys/RBAC tokens; cross-organization PII only
SQL injection is qualifying, but live login/API probing is not authorized until exact scope and proof boundary are approved
out_of_scope_or_high_risk: Customer data planes such as *.edge.gateways.<program-redacted>.com, *.kongcloud.io, customer proxy instances
Marketing/docs/support limits and conflicting <program-redacted>.com entries require careful exact asset normalization before any live action
Non-<program-slug> plugins and non-<program-slug> GitHub repositories
Sandbox functionality listed out of scope

candidate_lane_split.why: Executable/source-like components can be reviewed or locally reproduced without live target contact first.
Electron/Node CLI surfaces may allow dependency/source review and local marker proofs.
High bounty SaaS access-control target, but requires exact scope approval, account boundary, and no token generation without approval.
OIDC/OAuth focus is high-value but must stop before auth abuse/token handling.

next_step: Normalize exact assets and pick a local/source-first candidate, or ask operator for exact live scope approval if pursuing Konnect/API owned-account testing.
